stdout:
**Acceptance Criteria**

No blocking findings.

- `jit issue claim` dependency-blocked errors now include an explicit `jit issue assign <id> <assignee>` remediation in human output via `TransitionBlockedError::remediation_commands()` at [crates/jit/src/errors.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/errors.rs:275).
- The same remediation flows into JSON `error.suggestions` and `error.details.remediation` through [crates/jit/src/output.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/output.rs:478).
- `jit issue claim <id> <assignee> --assign-only` is parsed at [crates/jit/src/cli.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/cli.rs:548) and delegates to `assign_issue` without calling the normal claim state transition path at [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:1173).
- Documentation covers the new assign/claim distinction and `--assign-only` at [docs/reference/cli-commands.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/reference/cli-commands.md:984).

**Architecture and Separation of Concerns**

No blocking findings.

- The new flag remains in CLI parsing, while mutation uses the existing command-layer `assign_issue` orchestration at [crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:554).
- Persistence remains behind `IssueStore`; the CLI does not write files directly.

**Functional Paradigm**

No blocking findings.

- The remediation construction uses iterator chaining rather than imperative accumulation at [crates/jit/src/errors.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/errors.rs:296).
- The new CLI branch is pragmatic command dispatch and output handling.

**Correctness and Invariants**

No blocking findings.

- Normal claim semantics still transition ready issues through `update_issue_state` before assignment at [crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/commands/issue.rs:607).
- `--assign-only` reuses assignment and does not transition state at [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:1182).
- Issue persistence still uses atomic temp-file plus rename writes at [crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:103).
- No PID-handling code was touched.

**Safety**

No blocking findings.

- `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` remains enforced in both the library and binary at [crates/jit/src/lib.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/lib.rs:6) and [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:14).
- I found no unsafe code introduced.

**Testing**

No blocking findings.

- New coverage directly exercises human blocked-claim output, JSON blocked-claim output, assign-only no-transition behavior, and normal claim transition behavior at [crates/jit/tests/exit_code_tests.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/tests/exit_code_tests.rs:823).
- Current cargo-ci gate evidence for this issue reports: `fmt: ok`, `clippy: ok`, `test: 2219 passed, 0 failed, 12 ignored`.
- Per instruction, I did not rerun cargo tests because the issue has a succeeded cargo-ci gate.

**Error Handling**

No blocking findings.

- Blocked transition remains a typed `TransitionBlockedError`; JSON handling still downcasts to structured blocked output at [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:1207).
- New test code uses `unwrap()`, but only in tests.

**CLI Contract**

No blocking findings.

- `claim --assign-only --json` returns machine-readable success output at [crates/jit/src/main.rs](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/crates/jit/src/main.rs:1190).
- Existing `claim --json` blocked output remains structured and now includes the new remediation.

**Documentation**

No blocking findings.

- User-facing command reference documents `jit issue assign`, `jit issue claim`, `--assign-only`, and dependency-blocked remediation at [docs/reference/cli-commands.md](/home/vkaskivuo/Projects/just-in-time/docs/reference/cli-commands.md:984).
